name: Quinoa
summary: A gluten-free pseudo-grain that's a complete plant protein — all nine essential amino acids — plus whole-grain fiber and minerals.
superfoodReason: One of the few plant staples that's a complete protein, cooked and eaten like a whole grain, and naturally gluten-free.

benefits.mechanism: Most grains are low in the amino acid lysine, but quinoa supplies all nine essential amino acids in usable amounts — so it counts as a complete protein for building and maintaining muscle, unusual for a plant food.

citations.title: Protein — The Nutrition Source, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

benefits.mechanism: Eaten as a whole grain, quinoa provides fiber, magnesium, and iron. Higher whole-grain intake is consistently linked to lower risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and premature death.

citations.title: Carbohydrate quality and human health: a series of systematic reviews and meta-analyses (Reynolds et al., Lancet 2019)

surprisingFacts.text: Quinoa isn't a true cereal grain — it's the seed of a plant related to spinach and beets. Rinse it before cooking to wash off the bitter saponin coating that protects the seeds from birds.

cautions.effect: A sudden jump in fibre brings bloating, gas and looser stools until your gut adjusts — increase the amount gradually and drink enough water. Rinsing removes the bitter saponin coating.
